summary: Keep the published CLI entrypoint from importing behavior subsystems at module import time.
rationale: This slice preserves the existing CLI command surface while moving runtime, provider, evaluation, corpus, flywheel, workbench, and live-smoke behavior imports behind command execution wrappers. The CLI remains a parser/router boundary instead of eagerly loading production subsystems on import.
verification: uv run pytest tests/test_cli_import_boundaries.py tests/test_cli_basic_commands.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_cli_packaging.py tests/test_api.py tests/test_evaluation.py -q
uv run ruff check src/seektalent/__init__.py src/seektalent/version.py src/seektalent/cli.py src/seektalent/cli_basic_commands.py tests/test_cli_import_boundaries.py
uv run ty check src tests tools
uv run pytest tests/test_liepin_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_opencli_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_cli.py tests/test_liepin_boundaries.py -q
deletion_targets: top-level CLI imports of runtime execution, provider, evaluation, corpus, flywheel, workbench, and live-smoke behavior subsystems
package initializer eager imports that load API/runtime dependencies before the CLI module can stay lightweight
published CLI tests depending on eager behavior imports instead of stable command execution seams
risks: The existing run_match and remote-eval monkeypatch seams are intentionally preserved as lazy wrappers for current tests and downstream command extensions.
This slice does not move all command bodies out of cli.py; UI server and CTS client decomposition are separate Task 9 stacked PRs.
